description_long: This object is a flintlock-style handgun with a long, slender barrel and a dark, ornately carved wooden grip. The grip features subtle decorative patterns, giving it a classic, antique appearance. The metal components, including the barrel, trigger, and hammer, have a matte, slightly weathered finish, suggesting age and historical use. The trigger guard is thin and elegantly curved, complementing the overall vintage design. No modern features such as a slide or magazine are visible, reinforcing its historical character. The object is compact and lightweight, suitable for handheld use in close-quarters scenarios.
description: A vintage flintlock handgun with a long barrel, dark carved wooden grip, and matte metal components, exuding an antique aesthetic.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, ornate wooden grip, and detailed trigger mechanism.
description_view_1: Front-on view highlighting the circular barrel opening and the narrow profile of the grip and frame.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the barrel, grip, and hammer, with visible wood grain and metal finish.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the grip and the alignment of the barrel, showing the grip's contour and pattern.
attribute_grip_design: The grip is crafted from dark wood with a gently curved, ergonomic shape that fits comfortably in the hand. It features subtle carved patterns along its surface, adding both visual interest and a slight tactile texture. The wood appears polished but not glossy, with no visible finger grooves or logos. The contouring is smooth, and the grip tapers slightly toward the base, enhancing control and stability.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is thin, rounded, and elegantly curved, following the traditional style of antique flintlock pistols. It is made of metal, with a smooth finish and no visible texturing or reinforcement. The guard is minimalistic, lacking any undercuts, squared-off edges, or integrated accessories, and is proportionate to the overall size of the handgun.
attribute_barrel_type: The barrel is long and slender, with a smooth, round profile and no visible threading, porting, or external features such as compensators. It has a matte, slightly weathered finish, consistent with aged steel, and no visible markings or embellishments. The length and style are characteristic of historical flintlock pistols.
attribute_slide_markings: There are no visible slide markings, engravings, or inscriptions on this handgun, as it lacks a modern slide mechanism. The metal surfaces are plain, with only subtle decorative elements on the wooden grip.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: There is no visible magazine base or floorplate, as this handgun is a single-shot flintlock design without a detachable magazine. The base of the grip is smoothly rounded and unadorned, with no extensions, bevels, or finger rests.
